raw_response:
Arch perimeter refers to the overall outline of the dental arch, encompassing the maxillary and/or mandibular teeth and surrounding structures. None of the articles directly mention "arch perimeter" being measured in particular instruments like those listed in the options. However, given the nature of cephalometric analyses described extensively throughout the materials, we can infer that tools capable of capturing comprehensive images would facilitate such a measurement indirectly. \boxed{A} Cephalogram captures detailed lateral views suitable for assessing the spatial arrangement and morphology of the whole craniomaxillofacial region, thus allowing for indirect measurement of components contributing to arch perimeter.
This conclusion leverages the extensive description of how cephalograms support broader assessments related to craniofacial structure and function rather than directly stating arch perimeter quantification.
